project.name: GitHub Task Manager - Web Application
project.description: Collaborative task management system integrated with GitHub with automatic task ID generation, template validation, multi-project support, and deterministic commit tracking. Current state: core features complete, commit format standardized (TaskDB|action|id|name|summary). Future: validation CI, metrics collection, and dashboard enhancements.

graph_end.summary: This graph closes the current delivery scope, then returns the system to its ongoing maintenance, validation, and contributor-support cycle.
graph_end.next_step: After the terminal delivery tasks complete, shift into release health checks, issue triage, contributor feedback, and the next planning iteration.
graph_end.success_signal: Terminal tasks are shipped, documentation is current, and the live system can be monitored without reopening the same delivery chain.
graph_end.maintenance_rhythm: Review deployment health, validation scripts, and incoming issues each iteration before opening the next delivery graph.

tasks.description: Establish secure token handling, stabilize the local dev and E2E test environment, align task data files, review the public token security model, and archive the legacy task file. These foundational steps unlock all downstream delivery.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub token loaded securely from config, not from UI input
Local dev and Playwright tests run without hitting production
tasks.csv and node.tasks.json are in sync
Token security model documented and validated
Legacy root task file archived without modifying canonical database

subtasks.description: Remove the personal token input section from the UI. Implement pre-configured GitHub token in config to allow public users to collaborate. Token should be stored securely in environment variables for GitHub Pages deployment.

comments.author: public@example.com
comments.text: Need to ensure this works with GitHub Actions secrets

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Completed - Token is now loaded from github-token.js which is gitignored. For production, GitHub Actions injects the token from secrets.

subtasks.task_name: Stabilize Local Dev & E2E Test Setup
subtasks.description: Fix local dev/test issues: make token loading safe when no injected variable exists, point the app to the deployed tasks database file, and stabilize Playwright to run locally without hitting production.
Update /public/tasksDB/tasks.csv so it reflects the same project state as /public/tasksDB/node.tasks.json (statuses, dates, and any newly added tasks).
Confirm the intended security model for public collaboration (token injection, permissions/scoping, abuse prevention). Document the approach and constraints in README/tools docs.

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Added README guidance and GH Actions note; next steps: finalize token model and add deployment docs.

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Completed: token model documented, .gitignore updated for derived files, CI step design ready.

subtasks.description: Preserve the older repo-root node.tasks.json as an immutable history snapshot under /public/tasksDB/history without changing the canonical runtime database.

tasks.description: Build the complete task form with all required fields, implement task automation logic, add subtask and parent-child support, define dependencies with critical path detection, enable public collaboration, and add clickable dashboard stat cards.
acceptance_criteria: All schema fields have form inputs with validation
task_id, created_date, creator_id auto-populated on create
Parent-child task relationships supported via parent_task_id
Dependency types FS/SS/FF/SF with circular cycle detection
Public collaboration with comment system working
Dashboard stat cards clickable for status filtering

subtasks.task_name: Update Task Form with All Required Fields
subtasks.description: Add form inputs for all non-automatable task fields from template schema. Include fields for: task_name, description, status, priority, start_date, end_date, estimated_hours, category_name, tags, assigned_workers, dependencies, parent_task_id, is_critical_path. Auto-populate task_id, created_date, and creator_id.

subtasks.task_name: Add text inputs for task_name and description
Add dropdowns for status, priority, category
Add date pickers for start_date and end_date
Add tags input with chip UI
Add dependency selector with type dropdown

acceptance_criteria: All schema fields have corresponding form inputs
task_id, created_date, creator_id are auto-populated
Validation errors show before submission
Form resets after successful creation

subtasks.description: Enhance template-automation.js to automatically populate task_id, created_date, creator_id, and other automatable fields. Ensure validation happens before task creation. Support status normalization and default values from TEMPLATE_CONFIG.

Implement parent_task_id functionality to enable subtask creation. Update the UI to show parent-child relationships and support creating subtasks from task detail view. Ensure dependencies work correctly with subtasks.
Add UI controls for defining task dependencies with types (FS, SS, FF, SF) and lag_days. Implement critical path visualization. Validate dependency chains to prevent circular dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency type selector supports FS, SS, FF, SF
Lag days field accepts positive integers
Circular dependency detection prevents invalid chains
Critical path highlighted visually on the graph
Dependency links render correctly between nodes

subtasks.description: Add user name input for public collaborators. Implement comment system on tasks. Add worker assignment without authentication. Enable status updates and progress tracking for all users. Show who made changes and when.
Make the dashboard stat cards clickable so users can quickly filter tasks by status (All, Not Started, In Progress, Completed).

tasks.description: Add password protection for all modification operations, create a timeline/Gantt visualization, implement two-way GitHub Issues sync, and expose an MCP endpoint for marking critical path tasks. This phase completes the core feature surface.
acceptance_criteria: View-only operations work without password; create/edit/delete blocked without correct password
Timeline/Gantt view renders task date axis with status colours and dependency lines
Two-way GitHub Issues sync creates/updates issues from tasks and vice versa
MCP endpoint accepts POST and marks tasks as critical path

subtasks.description: Implement a simple password gate for all modification actions (create, edit, delete tasks). Users can view tasks and dashboard freely, but must enter a shared password before making changes. GitHub Pages uses a repo Secret injected at deploy time; local dev is unlocked by default.

subtasks.task_name: Wire password check to all mutation actions
Add config toggle for local dev bypass

acceptance_criteria: View-only operations work without password
Create/edit/delete blocked without correct password
Password persists in sessionStorage
Local dev unlocked by default

subtasks.description: Add a visual timeline/Gantt-style view showing tasks on a date axis. Display task start/end dates, status colors, dependencies as connecting lines. Allow toggle between list view and timeline view. Show clear distinction between completed and pending tasks.

Two-way sync between tasks and GitHub Issues. Create issues from tasks, import issues as tasks. Keep status synchronized. Show issue number and link on task cards. Use GitHub API to read/write issues.

Create a new API endpoint /api/create-mcp that accepts a POST request with a list of task IDs and marks them as critical path tasks (is_critical_path=true). Validate input, update node.tasks.json, and return success/failure response.

tasks.description: Comprehensive testing of the task creation flow, E2E tests for password, timeline, and GitHub Issues, and deployment to GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions with environment secrets injection.
acceptance_criteria: All task creation field types tested including auto-population
E2E tests cover password protection, timeline, and GitHub Issues sync
GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main
Token injected from repo secrets at deploy time
Public URL serves the app and all CRUD operations work on live deployment

subtasks.description: Comprehensive testing of task creation flow with all field types. Verify auto-population of task_id, dates, and creator. Test validation error messages. Test with various input combinations.

Create Playwright E2E tests for: 1) Password protection flow (blocked without password, allowed with password), 2) Timeline view rendering and interaction, 3) GitHub Issues sync operations. Test locally before deploying.
Setup GitHub Actions workflow to deploy the task manager to GitHub Pages. Configure environment variables for GitHub token. Test public access and collaboration features on live deployment.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main
Token injected from repo secrets at deploy time
Public URL serves the app correctly
All CRUD operations work on live deployment

tasks.description: Implement automated CI checks for commit format compliance, create a schema validation script for all node.tasks.json files, and standardize validation/enrichment tooling under consistent naming conventions.
acceptance_criteria: validate-commit-format.js parses git log and flags non-compliant commits with line number
GitHub Actions workflow runs commit validation on PR and nightly
validate-tasks-schema.js validates all node.tasks.json required fields and date ordering
Scripts standardised under validate-tasks-workers / enrich-tasks-workers naming

subtasks.description: Add automated CI checks to validate all commits match the TaskDB|action|id|name|summary format. Create tools/scripts/validate-commit-format.js that parses recent commits and enforces the structured subject format. Add GitHub Actions workflow to run on PRs and nightly. Report compliance metrics.
acceptance_criteria: validate-commit-format.js parses git log output
Non-compliant commits flagged with line number
GitHub Actions workflow runs on PR and nightly
Compliance percentage reported in CI output

subtasks.description: Create tools/scripts/validate-tasks-schema.js that validates both projects' node.tasks.json against required fields, date ordering, and no timestamp pollution in task_name. Ensure all required fields (task_id, task_name, description, start_date, end_date, priority, status, estimated_hours, category_name) are present. Run on every save and add to CI.

Standardize validation/enrichment tooling under tools/scripts/validate-tasks-workers.js and tools/scripts/enrich-tasks-workers.js; keep old script names as deprecated wrappers for backwards compatibility.

tasks.description: Write comprehensive documentation for public users and developers, conduct a project retrospective, remove legacy terminology from roadmap artifacts, and document the task source audit workflow and output artifact paths.
acceptance_criteria: Comprehensive README and contributing guide published for public users and developers
Project retrospective findings documented
Legacy terminology removed from roadmap artifacts
Git history workflow and temporary output path conventions documented

subtasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation for public users on how to use the task manager. Include examples of creating tasks, managing dependencies, setting status, and collaborating. Add developer setup guide for GitHub Pages deployment.

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Added contributing guidance on generated task files and workflow; more docs pending.

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Added CONTRIBUTING.md draft and npm regeneration scripts to package.json.

subtasks.description: Conduct project retrospective to review what went well, what could be improved, and lessons learned. Document findings and create improvement tasks for next iteration.

comments.author: dev@example.com
comments.text: Kickoff: captured conflicts and workstream improvements (ignore derived files; rebase workflow).

subtasks.task_name: Remove Legacy Terminology from Roadmap Artifacts
subtasks.description: Update roadmap plan/docs and task metadata to use neutral terms (contributors/unassigned). Rename legacy capacity field names to assignee_capacity via enrichment so task data matches the project concept.

subtasks.task_name: Document task source audit workflow and output artifact paths
subtasks.description: Capture the recommended Git history and blame workflow for public/tasksDB/external/github-task-manager/node.tasks.json, and document that temporary outputs such as tmp-test-output.txt, tmp-calendar-run.txt, and live-test-run.log belong under outputs/local-runs/ instead of the repo root.

subtasks.name: Add git history command examples to README
Document which outputs are temporary and should live outside source directories
Capture what current task and git status means for the project state

tasks.description: Implement node visual states (done/in-progress animations), add in-graph task editing, popup dropdown sections for acceptance criteria and subtasks, inline subtask drill-down navigation with parent/successor nav, priority-coloured dependency buttons, a tasks.json format validation test suite, and stabilise the graph renderer and Playwright base URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Done tasks render greyed/desaturated; In Progress tasks show pulsing aura animation
Edit button in graph node modals opens pre-populated form with password gate
Popups show acceptance_criteria and subtasks as collapsible dropdowns with count badges
Recursive inline subtask drill-down with breadcrumbs, parent nav, and successor nav
Dependency and subtask buttons display with priority colour and status animations
tasks.json format validation test suite passes for all project folders
All graph popup navigation buttons unified through shared handler with configured baseURL

subtasks.description: Update graph node rendering so nodes reflect task status: Completed tasks render greyed/desaturated with reduced opacity; In Progress tasks show a pulsing/irradiated aura (CSS animation + SVG glow). Not Started tasks remain static. Apply changes to the graph-display component and list nodes, add CSS variables and a feature toggle. Include unit and E2E tests to verify visual states.

subtasks.task_name: Add CSS classes for Done/In-Progress/Not-Started states
Implement pulse animation for In-Progress nodes
Implement greyed/desaturated style for Done nodes
Write E2E tests for visual states

acceptance_criteria: Done tasks render greyed/desaturated
In Progress tasks show pulsing aura animation
Not Started tasks remain static default styling
CSS variables allow customizing visual states
Feature toggle enables/disables status rendering
E2E test verifies each visual state

subtasks.task_name: Graph Task Editing UI (Edit from Graph Modal)
subtasks.description: Add an Edit button to graph node modals to open a pre-populated task edit form. Edits should respect the password protection gate and save back to the runtime store. Update `graph-display` to support in-place editing flow, add unit tests for modal behavior, and add Playwright E2E tests to verify edit/update persistence and UI gating.

subtasks.task_name: Popup Dropdown Menus for Acceptance Criteria & Subtasks
subtasks.description: Add collapsible <details> dropdown sections inside node popup modals to display acceptance_criteria and subtasks arrays from node.tasks.json. Each dropdown shows a count badge, styled with .popup-dropdown component. Acceptance criteria render as bullet lists; subtasks show name, status, and estimated hours.

subtasks.name: Add acceptance_criteria dropdown HTML to buildTaskManagementTemplate
Add subtasks dropdown HTML to buildTaskManagementTemplate
Create .popup-dropdown CSS component
Add unit tests for dropdown rendering

acceptance_criteria: Popup shows acceptance_criteria as collapsible dropdown with count badge
Popup shows subtasks as collapsible dropdown with name/status/hours per subtask
Dropdowns use <details class='popup-dropdown'> component
Empty arrays produce no dropdown section

subtasks.task_name: Inline Subtask Drill-Down Navigation with Parent & Successor Nav
subtasks.description: Enable recursive drill-down into inline subtasks directly from node popup. Clicking '📂 View Subtasks' on a task that has subtasks[] in node.tasks.json builds a temporary subgraph from those subtasks. The subgraph start node shows a '↩ Parent Task Name' button that navigates back to the parent graph and opens the parent task modal. The subgraph end node shows 'Next tasks in parent graph' buttons for successor tasks. Breadcrumbs track navigation depth. Supports recursive nesting (subtasks within subtasks).

subtasks.name: Build buildInlineSubgraphData to convert subtasks into temporary graph data
Build resolveTaskSubtaskTargets with priority: inline > explicit > none
Add parent context to subtaskStack entries (parentTaskNodeId, parentSuccessors)
Implement start-node parent-nav button with openNodeModal after navigateToDepth
Implement end-node successor-nav buttons
Remove broken virtual sidebar modules and Task Sub-graphs folder
Add Playwright E2E tests for recursive drill-down and parent/sibling nav

acceptance_criteria: Clicking View Subtasks on a task with inline subtasks builds a subgraph and renders it in place
Breadcrumbs show the navigation path to the current subgraph level
Start node of subgraph has a back-to-parent button that navigates up and opens parent task modal
End node of subgraph shows successor task buttons that navigate up and open successor modal
Recursive drill-down works (subtask of subtask of subtask)
No broken 'Task Sub-graphs' sidebar folder or virtual module entries
resolveTaskSubtaskTargets prioritizes inline subtasks over explicit paths

subtasks.description: Create a comprehensive test suite that walks all public/tasksDB/ project folders recursively, discovers every node.tasks.json file, and validates format correctness: valid JSON, project metadata, task required fields, enum values, dependency integrity, subtask/acceptance_criteria array format, date formats, and duplicate detection. Provides actionable feedback for any format issues found.

subtasks.name: Implement recursive node.tasks.json file discovery
Implement validateTasksJson with all field checks
Add per-project dynamic test generation
Add graph-builder integration test (subtask targets link correctly)

acceptance_criteria: Test suite discovers all node.tasks.json files under public/tasksDB/ recursively
Validates JSON parsing, project metadata, task structure, enums, dates, dependencies
Detects duplicate task_ids and task_names
Validates subtasks[] and acceptance_criteria[] array format
Tests run on github-task-manager and test-tasks projects
Provides clear error messages with file path and field location

subtasks.task_name: Priority-Colored Dependency & Subtask Buttons with Status Animations
subtasks.description: Enhance popup modal rendering so Depends on buttons and Sub-task buttons display with the same priority color as their referenced nodes. In-progress items pulse/blink, done items are faded with line-through, not-started remain static. Sub-task buttons are rendered as node-like pill elements with hours badge. Start node details replaced hardcoded graph legend with dynamic project summary (task count, status, timeline). Category-based sidebar modules auto-generated for flat projects without navigation.modules config.

subtasks.name: Replace hardcoded start node graph legend with dynamic project summary
Add priority/status data attributes to dep-link buttons in graph-data.js
Render subtasks as node-like pill buttons with color/status/hours
Enrich subtask data from parent task fields
Apply priority colors and status CSS classes in showNodeDetails
Generate category-based sidebar modules for flat projects
Add CSS for dep-status-done, dep-status-in-progress, subtask-node-btn, btn-pulse, category-dimmed

acceptance_criteria: Depends on buttons show the priority color of the referenced task node
Depends on buttons for done tasks are faded with line-through
Depends on buttons for in-progress tasks pulse with box-shadow animation
Sub-tasks rendered as colored pill buttons with priority color background
Sub-tasks show hours badge when available
Sub-task buttons pulse if in-progress, fade if done
Start node popup shows project description, task count, status, timeline instead of hardcoded graph legend
Flat projects without navigation.modules get category-based sidebar entries
Category sidebar items highlight matching tasks and dim non-matching ones
Subtask data enriched from parent: priority, status, category, assigned worker inherited when not set

subtasks.task_name: Unify graph relation buttons and stabilize Playwright base URLs
subtasks.description: Removed the remaining subtask-dive button split by rendering graph popup relation buttons through the shared task-node-btn path, unified their click handling for subgraph/parent/sibling navigation, updated the graph regression selectors, and fixed local E2E tests to follow Playwright's configured baseURL instead of hardcoded localhost:3000. Also replaced the stale module-navigation color expectation with a theme-variable-backed assertion, aligned CRUD/update-task/fullscreen Playwright expectations with the current Done status and modal field IDs, and restored a stable data-task-id hook for UI automation.

subtasks.name: Replace leftover subtask-dive modal buttons with task-node-btn variants
Route related graph popup buttons through one shared navigation handler
Update graph and E2E tests to use configured baseURL and current theme expectations
Refresh stale local E2E selectors and assertions for current task card and modal markup

tasks.description: Extend the calendar exporter with pending/full variants and organised export folders, document the IndexedDB cache and FS write-back architecture, restrict local folder discovery to canonical node.tasks.json, run a periodic project health review, retire the duplicate bridge package, and move the doc-only API surface into docs.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar exporter generates both full and pending variants under sorted output folders
IndexedDB cache, FS write-back, and navigation performance architecture documented
Local folder discovery restricted to node.tasks.json canonical filename only
Periodic module health review completed with findings captured as follow-up tasks
Duplicate task bridge package retired or shim documented
Documentation-only public/api surface moved to docs with compatibility note

comments.author: ai@example.com
comments.text: Maintenance follow-up restored list-display parity after the phase regrouping: nested tasks now appear through All Tasks and Layer Tasks modes, the list detail popup was standardized onto the taskNodeModal pattern with graph-style relation buttons and View Subtasks navigation, and the focused module-navigation plus CRUD Playwright regressions were refreshed and passed. Remaining UX follow-up is to reassess whether the current phase grouping best reflects the real project structure once the regression cycle is fully settled.

subtasks.task_name: Add pending/full calendar variants and organize export folders
subtasks.description: Extend the calendar parser with a first-class task-scope option so it can generate both full and pending task calendars, write sorted project calendars under calendars/all and calendars/pending, nest worker variants under workers-calendar/all and workers-calendar/pending, remove the legacy workers-calendar-pending layout, and keep the custom unit runner aligned while validating the change.

subtasks.name: Add a parser option to choose all, pending, or both calendar variants
Write project and worker calendar variants into sorted output folders
Update calendar docs and package scripts for the new export modes
Validate the parser and repair the custom test runner filename fallback

subtasks.task_name: Document IndexedDB Cache, FS Write-Back, and Instant Navigation Architecture
subtasks.description: Document the new architecture for local-folder TaskDB projects: IndexedDB-backed FolderCache for instant load, file hash-based change detection, and browser FS write-back. Detail how navigation performance was improved by pre-positioning nodes and removing unnecessary stabilization waits. Include affected modules (folder-cache.js, local-folder-scanner.js, task-storage-sync.js, main-graph.js), test coverage, and validation steps.

comments.author: ai@example.com
comments.text: Added comprehensive documentation of the new cache/write-back architecture and navigation performance improvements. All tests passing.

acceptance_criteria: Project state file documents IndexedDB cache and FS write-back for local-folder projects
Navigation logic improvements (pre-positioning, instant modal) are described
Affected modules and test coverage are listed
Validation steps and outcomes are included

subtasks.task_name: Restrict Local Folder Discovery to node.tasks.json and Index Project Paths Read-Only
subtasks.description: Update the local-folder import flow used by Open Local Folder and Browse Folder so discovery is scoped to the canonical node.tasks.json filename only, legacy TODO_project_task.json is ignored, and the project folder structure is indexed as lightweight path metadata without reading non-task file contents.
acceptance_criteria: Local folder discovery accepts node.tasks.json as canonical task file and ignores legacy candidates
Folder scans retain a lightweight directory/file path index without parsing non-task file contents
Picker feedback explicitly states that paths are indexed and only node.tasks.json files are read

subtasks.task_name: Periodic Project Review for Module Health, Issues, and Optimization Opportunities
subtasks.description: Add a near-terminal review node that runs after the remaining documentation, retrospective, graph-editing, API, calendar, and local-folder maintenance work. Walk project modules and delivery surfaces, look for bugs, stale assumptions, performance bottlenecks, and refactor opportunities, then turn findings into concrete follow-up tasks or close the cycle when no further action is needed.
acceptance_criteria: Review covers main project modules including list-display, graph-display, local-folder, task-engine, API, and project data flows
Potential issues, regressions, or optimization opportunities documented as follow-up tasks

subtasks.description: Replace the vendored task bridge copy under public/local-folder/task-bridge-modules/taskbridge with an explicit reference or compatibility shim to the canonical tools/task-bridge module so bridge server maintenance only happens in one place.
acceptance_criteria: tools/task-bridge remains the only maintained bridge implementation
Any remaining public/local-folder bridge path becomes a documented shim or is removed
Bridge startup and integration flows still work after the duplicate package is retired

subtasks.task_name: Move doc-only public/api surface into docs with an explicit compatibility note
subtasks.description: Relocate the public/api documentation-only folder into a docs-oriented location so the project no longer presents a runtime-looking API folder that only contains README content, while leaving a small compatibility note or redirect if existing links still depend on the current path.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation moves to a docs-oriented path instead of staying under a runtime-looking public/api folder
Any existing incoming references still land on a clear compatibility note or redirect

tasks.description: Pilot the manifest.json convention for self-describing Agentic IDE units, unblock validation when optional local model assets are absent, add a root server/ launch surface for the bridge and inference runtime, expand manifest coverage across more units, and extend the folder contract pattern beyond the Agentic IDE pilot into shared repo modules.
acceptance_criteria: manifest.schema.json documents the folder contract; pilot manifests pass the validator
validate:agentic-cells completes in a clean checkout without requiring absent local model assets
public/agentic-ide/server/ provides an explicit bridge launch entry with schema, manifest, and health test
Manifest coverage expanded to additional clean Agentic IDE units visible in registry.json
At least one shared runtime or UI module outside Agentic IDE adopts the folder contract pattern

subtasks.task_name: Pilot manifest.json convention for self-describing Agentic IDE units
subtasks.description: Add a manifest schema plus first-pass manifest.json files for already self-contained Agentic IDE folders so each pilot unit can declare its entry file, contract file, tests, and explicit workspace relations without changing the runtime loading model yet.

comments.author: ai@example.com
comments.text: Added manifest.schema.json, created pilot manifests, updated validator, documented in README.

acceptance_criteria: public/agentic-ide/schema/manifest.schema.json exists and documents the manifest contract
Pilot units expose manifest.json files with entry, contract, test, and relation metadata
The Agentic IDE validator recognizes manifests and publishes manifest coverage into registry.json

subtasks.task_name: Unblock full validate:agentic-cells runs when optional local model assets are absent
subtasks.description: Resolve the current Agentic IDE validation blocker where validate-agentic-cells fails when a local GGUF model file is not present in the repo checkout. Decided to treat that asset as optional and downgrade absent files to warnings.

comments.author: ai@example.com
comments.text: Updated model validation to honor model_asset_path and downgrade absent local GGUF assets to warnings; validate:agentic-cells now completes successfully.

acceptance_criteria: validate-agentic-cells can run to completion in a clean checkout without requiring large local model artifacts
The chosen behavior for optional model assets is documented clearly for maintainers and CI

subtasks.task_name: Add root server/ launch surface for Agentic IDE bridge and inference
subtasks.description: Create a root-level public/agentic-ide/server folder with a structured launch entry, contract file, manifest, health test definition, and README so the Agentic IDE bridge and inference runtime can be launched from an explicit server surface.

comments.author: ai@example.com
comments.text: Added public/agentic-ide/server/{main.js,schema.json,manifest.json,tests/health.json,README.md}, switched preferred bridge launch path to server/main.js.

The server folder contains schema.json, manifest.json, tests/health.json, and README.md

subtasks.description: Continue the manifest rollout from the first four pilot units into the rest of the clean Agentic IDE tools, agents, subgraphs, and workflows so more folders become self-describing and registry coverage can be used as a real progress measure.

subtasks.task_name: Extend manifest/schema/main/tests folder contract across non-Agentic project folders
subtasks.description: Take the folder-structure example beyond Agentic IDE and apply the same manifest/schema/main/tests discipline to the rest of the repo where it makes sense, starting with graph-display, task-engine, task-bridge, calendar, and other self-contained modules.
